summary.name: Daily SPY Range-Asymmetry Classifier
summary.author: Sheikh Pancham
summary.description: The strategy trades a single US equity index proxy (SPY) using a daily price action classifier intended to capture short term continuation or reversal patterns from the prior session. Each trading day it evaluates yesterday s OHLC bar and compares the distance from the open to the high versus the open to the low. summary.description_full:
The strategy trades a single US equity index proxy (SPY) using a daily price action classifier intended to capture short term continuation or reversal patterns from the prior session. Each trading day it evaluates yesterday s OHLC bar and compares the distance from the open to the high versus the open to the low. If the close is above the open and the upside excursion dominates the downside (high minus open greater than open minus low), it signals a 1 day long. If the close is below the open, it typically goes long when downside excursion dominates (interpreted as rejection and potential mean reversion) and goes short when upside excursion dominates (interpreted as failed rally). Doji like bars can result in flat or directional signals depending on the same excursion comparison.

Signals are generated once per day from daily OHLC data and expressed as 1 day horizons. Portfolio construction applies equal weighting across the active universe, which here is only SPY, implying full allocation to the current long short or flat view. The primary risk control is the short forecast horizon with daily refresh; there are no explicit volatility targeting, stop loss, leverage, or exposure caps beyond the single instrument and rebalancing cadence. The intended edge is systematic exploitation of intraday range asymmetry relative to the open as a proxy for supply demand imbalance and exhaustion.
